rationale: The citing paper uses Camerer (2005) in its theory development section on emotions, explicitly invoking that paper's claim that loss aversion can reflect an exaggerated emotional fear response. This supports the citing paper's core conceptual setup that losses trigger negative affect that people under-anticipate, leading to higher-than-planned subsequent bets. The citation is not used for Camerer's specific formal analysis or empirical setting, but it plays a meaningful framing role for the proposed mechanism.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Camerer (2005) as conceptual support for an emotion-based interpretation of loss aversion. It uses this idea to motivate its hypotheses and interpretation of why actual betting after losses exceeds planned betting.
